description: ClawHQ backlog — derived from PRODUCT.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, README.md (2026-03-18)

items.title: Config schema + 14 landmine validator
items.description: TypeScript types for OpenClawConfig, ClawHQConfig, DeploymentBundle, CronJobDefinition. Validator implementing all 14 landmine rules from OPENCLAW-REFERENCE.md. YAML config loader with precedence merging (project > user > defaults). This is the foundation everything else builds on.
items.groomingNotes: FOUNDATION: Every config-generating command in ClawHQ tests against this schema. Without it, config generation has no contract and silent landmines ship to users. The 14 landmine validator is the difference between 'it works' and 'it works correctly'. Acceptance: all 14 rules validate correctly against OPENCLAW-REFERENCE.md; config loader handles precedence merging (project > user > defaults).
items.intent: Establish the config contract that every config-generating command validates against. All 14 OpenClaw landmine rules must be enforced at the schema level so no silent misconfiguration ships to users.

items.title: Gateway WebSocket RPC client
items.description: Token-authenticated WebSocket client for OpenClaw Gateway. Request/response RPC with typed errors, timeout handling, AbortSignal support. Custom error classes (GatewayError, ConnectionError, AuthError, RateLimitError, RpcTimeoutError). Health polling with configurable interval and timeout.
items.groomingNotes: FOUNDATION: Every live operation — deploy health checks, config patches, session queries — goes through this client. Without it, ClawHQ can only write files; it cannot talk to a running agent. Acceptance: RPC round-trip works with real Gateway; timeout + abort signal paths exercised; typed error classes surface correct user messages.
items.intent: Give ClawHQ the ability to talk to a running OpenClaw agent. Every live operation depends on this RPC client — without it, ClawHQ can only write files, never observe or control a deployed agent.

items.title: Docker two-stage build + container hardening
items.description: Stage 1: base OpenClaw + apt packages (cached). Stage 2: custom tools + skills (fast rebuild). Hash-based change detection for Stage 1 invalidation. Build manifest with image hash, layers, size. Four security postures (minimal/standard/hardened/paranoid) with cap_drop ALL, read-only rootfs, non-root UID 1000, ICC disabled, resource limits per posture.
items.groomingNotes: BUILD GATE: No Docker build = no running agent. The two-stage architecture matters for user experience: Stage 1 rebuilds rarely (base image), Stage 2 rebuilds fast (custom tools/skills). Hash-based change detection means 'clawhq build' doesn't waste the user's time. Four security postures mean users get hardened-by-default without knowing what cap_drop means. Acceptance: build produces a runnable image; Stage 1 cache hit on no-change; security posture applied without user configuration.
items.intent: Deliver a two-stage Docker build that caches the base image and hardens every container to at least standard security posture by default. Users must never run an unhardened agent.

items.title: Deploy orchestration + preflight checks
items.description: Full deploy sequence: preflight -> compose up -> firewall apply -> health verify -> smoke test. 6 preflight checks (Docker, images, config, secrets, ports, Ollama). Graceful shutdown and restart with firewall reapply. AbortSignal support throughout. Step-by-step progress reporting via callback.
items.groomingNotes: USER MOMENT: This is what 'clawhq up' does — the user's first deploy experience. The full sequence (preflight -> compose -> firewall -> health -> smoke test) ensures the user gets a working agent or a clear error, never silent failure. Step-by-step progress reporting means the user isn't staring at a frozen terminal. Acceptance: 'clawhq up' produces a running agent; all 6 preflight checks surface actionable errors; health verify confirms agent is reachable before returning.
items.intent: Make 'clawhq up' produce a running, reachable agent or a clear, actionable error — never silent failure. Every preflight check must block deploy if it fails.

items.title: Egress firewall (iptables CLAWHQ_FWD chain)
items.description: Dedicated iptables chain: ESTABLISHED/RELATED -> DNS -> HTTPS to allowlisted domains only -> LOG + DROP. Per-integration domain allowlist derived from config. Air-gap mode (block all egress). Idempotent apply/remove. Verification with expected vs actual rule diff. Auto-reapply after docker compose down.
items.groomingNotes: PRIVACY PROMISE: The egress firewall is what makes 'zero data leaves your machine by default' true, not marketing. Without it, an agent tool or skill could call out to arbitrary domains. Per-integration domain allowlist means the user's email agent can reach email APIs and nothing else. Air-gap mode is the paranoid user's kill switch. Acceptance: docker bridge traffic blocked to non-allowlisted domains; DNS and HTTPS to allowlist work; firewall reapplied after compose down; 'clawhq doctor' verifies rules are active.
items.intent: Enforce that no egress traffic leaves the agent container except to explicitly allowlisted domains. The privacy promise is architectural, not advisory.

items.title: PII + secrets scanner
items.description: Pattern-based scanner for API keys, passwords, tokens, PII in workspace files. False-positive filtering (comments, placeholders, example.com). Redacted output. Recursive directory walk. Git history scanning.
items.groomingNotes: USER VALUE: Security hardening, not blocking adoption. Scanning existing workspace for PII is important but the agent runs without it. Promote back to must when install flow is complete and security pass begins.
items.intent: Surface any API keys, passwords, or PII in workspace files before deploy. False positives must not train users to ignore scan results.

items.title: Secrets management (.env store)
items.description: Parse/read/write .env files with format preservation. Atomic writes (temp + rename, 0600 permissions). Get/set/remove values.
items.groomingNotes: SECURITY BASELINE: If .env is misconfigured or readable by other processes, the user's API keys are exposed. Atomic writes (temp + rename) and 0600 permissions are non-negotiable. This is the lowest layer of the credentials stack — everything CQ-008 (health probes) does depends on reliable .env reads. Acceptance: written files are always 0600; atomic write survives process kill mid-write; get/set/remove preserve format and comments.
items.intent: Ensure every credential written to disk is atomic, 0600-permissioned, and survives process interruption. No partial writes, no world-readable secrets.

items.title: Credential health probes
items.description: Probe-based credential validation. Built-in probes for Anthropic, OpenAI, Telegram. Extensible probe interface. Report aggregation.
items.groomingNotes: SILENT FAILURE PREVENTION: Credential expiry is one of the top reasons agents stop working without explanation. A user who set up their Anthropic key three months ago has no idea it expired. Probes run on schedule and surface the problem before the next tool call silently fails. Built-in probes for Anthropic, OpenAI, Telegram cover the most common integrations. Acceptance: probe reports correct pass/fail for each integration; failed credential surfaces actionable message; 'clawhq creds' outputs clean table.
items.intent: Detect expired or invalid credentials before they cause silent agent failures. Every configured integration must report pass/fail with an actionable fix message.

items.title: Blueprint loader + validator
items.description: Load blueprints from YAML (configs/templates/). 70+ validation checks. Security baseline enforcement. Multiple load paths. templateToChoice() conversion for wizard.
items.groomingNotes: BLUEPRINT ENGINE FOUNDATION: The loader is the reading end of the blueprint system. Without it, 'clawhq blueprint list/preview' (FEAT-001) and the init wizard (CQ-010) have nothing to work with. 70+ validation checks mean a malformed community blueprint can't corrupt the user's setup. Acceptance: all built-in blueprints load cleanly; invalid YAML surfaces readable error; size limit prevents DoS on large files; templateToChoice() works correctly for wizard display.
items.intent: Load and validate any blueprint YAML — built-in or community — rejecting malformed input with clear errors. This is the read path the entire blueprint engine depends on.

items.title: Init wizard + config generation + writer
items.description: Interactive setup wizard. Config generation implementing all 14 landmine rules. Atomic file writer. Air-gapped mode support.
items.groomingNotes: CORE USER FLOW: This is the forging ceremony — the moment where a blueprint becomes a personalized agent. The guided wizard is the primary path for the Privacy Migrant persona who chose ClawHQ specifically because they don't want to hand-write 13,500 tokens of config. Air-gapped mode support ensures the wizard works without calling home. Acceptance: wizard completes without errors on happy path; generated config passes all 14 landmine rules; atomic writer never produces partial files; air-gap mode skips cloud-dependent steps gracefully.
items.intent: Guide a non-technical user from blueprint selection to a valid, landmine-free config in one interactive session. Generated config must pass all 14 validation rules.

items.title: Doctor diagnostics + auto-fix
items.description: 11+ preventive checks with auto-fix. Table + JSON output.
items.groomingNotes: HERO FEATURE: 'doctor' is the safety net that turns OpenClaw from 'it silently broke' to 'I know exactly what's wrong and how to fix it'. The 14+ checks cover every known failure mode preventively. Auto-fix means the user doesn't need to know what cap_drop is — they just run 'clawhq doctor --fix' and it works. Table output is the key UX: all checks visible at a glance, pass/fail clear, fix suggestions actionable. Acceptance: all 14+ checks run; auto-fix resolves common issues; table alignment clean; JSON output available for scripting.
items.intent: Make 'clawhq doctor' the single command that catches every known failure mode before it hits the user. Auto-fix must resolve common issues without requiring knowledge of internals.

items.title: Encrypted backup + restore
items.description: GPG-encrypted snapshots. SHA-256 integrity verification. Temp dir isolation. Post-restore doctor check.
items.groomingNotes: USER VALUE: Day-2 operational maturity. Encrypted backup is essential for production use but does not block first working agent. Users need a running agent before they need to snapshot it. Promote to must before first public release.
items.intent: Deliver encrypted, integrity-verified snapshots that restore a working agent state. Post-restore doctor check must confirm the restored agent is healthy.

items.title: CLI entry point + UX helpers
items.description: Commander.js flat CLI. Error handler. First-run detection. Spinner + formatError helpers.
items.groomingNotes: HIGH VALUE: Gate item — nothing runs without this. Commander.js flat CLI (AD-01), first-run detection, spinner/error UX helpers. Acceptance: clawhq --help lists all commands; error handler formats cleanly; first-run detects missing install.
items.intent: Ship the CLI entry point so every command is reachable, errors are human-readable, and first-run detects missing prerequisites. Nothing runs without this.

items.title: clawhq install — pre-req detection + scaffold
items.description: Detect Docker, Node.js >=20, Ollama. Create ~/.clawhq/ with clawhq.yaml. Scaffold subdirs.
items.groomingNotes: HIGH VALUE: This is the literal first command a user runs. Gate: Docker detected, Node >=20, Ollama running. Creates ~/.clawhq/ scaffold. Without this, nothing else works. Acceptance: 'clawhq install' exits 0, ~/.clawhq/ structure exists, clawhq.yaml written, user sees next-step guidance.
items.intent: Make 'clawhq install' the first command that works — detect prerequisites, scaffold ~/.clawhq/, and leave the user with clear next-step guidance.

items.title: clawhq install --from-source
items.description: Clone OpenClaw, two-stage build, store in ~/.clawhq/engine/.
items.groomingNotes: USER VALUE: Advanced zero-trust acquisition for security-paranoid users. Not on default install path. Blocked by CQ-014 and CQ-003 anyway. Build after trusted-cache install is working end-to-end.
items.intent: Enable zero-trust users to clone, audit, and build OpenClaw from source with the same install command. The from-source path must produce an identical engine artifact.

items.title: ~/.clawhq/ deployment directory migration
items.description: Configurable deployment root. Replace hardcoded ~/.openclaw/ paths. Migration command.
items.groomingNotes: USER VALUE: Internal path migration — replaces hardcoded ~/.openclaw/ with configurable ~/.clawhq/. User-invisible refactor. Does not block MVP. Do after install scaffold (CQ-014) is working.
items.intent: Replace all hardcoded ~/.openclaw/ paths with configurable ~/.clawhq/ deployment root. Existing installations must migrate without data loss.

items.title: Email Manager blueprint YAML
items.description: First use-case blueprint. Email + calendar + tasks. Skills: email-digest, morning-brief. Hardened security.
items.groomingNotes: HIGH VALUE: THE first use case — this is what we show in demos. Defines Email Manager end-to-end: email + calendar + tasks tools, email-digest + morning-brief skills, 15min inbox cron, 8am daily digest. Acceptance: clawhq blueprint preview email-manager shows complete design; forge from CQ-010 produces valid openclaw.json. Must pair with CQ-022 (email-digest skill).
items.intent: Deliver the Email Manager blueprint as the reference implementation — email triage, calendar, tasks, cron, hardened security. This is the demo-ready first use case.

items.title: Blueprint personalizer
items.description: customization_questions in YAML schema. Wizard asks 1-3 questions per blueprint.
items.groomingNotes: USER VALUE: Phase 2 personalization. Adds customization_questions to blueprint schema so wizard asks 1-3 tailored questions. Valuable but not blocking: Email Manager (CQ-017) must work end-to-end first. Build after CQ-010 + CQ-017 prove the forge pipeline.
items.intent: Blueprint wizard must ask 1-3 personalization questions per blueprint so forged agents reflect user-specific preferences, not generic defaults.

items.title: Identity file generators (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, etc.)
items.description: Generate identity files from blueprint. SOUL.md (not IDENTITY.md) per docs. Read-only at runtime. Token budget.
items.groomingNotes: IDENTITY IS THE PRODUCT: SOUL.md defines who the agent IS — its personality, communication style, boundaries. Without it, the user gets a generic OpenClaw instance, not a purpose-built agent. Read-only mount means the agent cannot modify its own personality at runtime (LM-12 prevention). SOUL.md (not IDENTITY.md) per current docs. Acceptance: SOUL.md generated from blueprint + wizard answers; AGENTS.md reflects correct tool/skill inventory; identity files mounted read-only in container; token budget respected.
items.intent: Generate SOUL.md and AGENTS.md from the blueprint so every forged agent has a distinct identity. Identity files must be read-only at runtime — agents cannot rewrite who they are.

items.title: Workspace tool generators
items.description: 7 CLI tool wrappers: email, tasks, todoist, ical, quote, tavily, todoist-sync.
items.groomingNotes: AGENT'S HANDS: Tools are what the agent can DO. An email agent without an email tool can't triage inbox. Blueprint-driven generation means tools are consistent with what the identity and skills expect. CLI wrappers (bash/python3) are thin — they call himalaya, caldav, etc. — so adding a new integration means writing a wrapper, not modifying the agent. Acceptance: all 7 tool wrappers generate correctly from blueprint config; generated tools executable in container; tool names match blueprint's tool references in AGENTS.md.
items.intent: Generate all 7 workspace tool wrappers from blueprint config so the agent can act on the world. An agent without its tools is decoration.

items.title: Skill lifecycle + vetting
items.description: Stage -> vet -> approve -> activate. Rollback snapshots. Security vetting with recalibrated patterns.
items.groomingNotes: CAPABILITY GATE: Skill lifecycle is what makes skills safe. Without it, installing a skill means manually copying files with no vetting, no rollback, no audit. The email-digest skill (CQ-022) must go through this pipeline. The URL trap fix is critical — malicious skills should not be able to call out to arbitrary hosts. Rollback snapshots mean 'clawhq skill install' is reversible. Acceptance: install flow runs stage->vet->approve->activate; vet catches URL patterns; rollback restores previous state; 'clawhq skill list' shows installed skills with status.
items.intent: Every skill installation must pass security vetting before activation. Rollback must restore the previous state if a skill breaks the agent.

items.title: Skill: email-digest
items.description: Triage inbox: categorize, summarize, propose responses. SKILL.md + prompts.
items.groomingNotes: HIGH VALUE: First autonomous capability the user sees. Pairs with CQ-017 (Email Manager blueprint). This skill is why the user chose ClawHQ over Gmail. Acceptance: SKILL.md + prompts installed; agent triages inbox, categorizes emails, proposes responses with approval gates; runs on 15min cron. No data leaves machine (local Ollama).
items.intent: Deliver the email-digest skill so the Email Manager agent triages inbox, categorizes, and proposes responses with approval gates. No auto-send, no data leaves the machine.

items.title: Audit trail (tool + egress + secrets)
items.description: Three audit systems. Append-only JSONL. HMAC-chained secret lifecycle. OWASP compliance export.
items.groomingNotes: TRANSPARENCY PROMISE: 'You know what your agent did, what data it touched, and what left your machine' — this is a design principle. Without audit trail, it's just a claim. The user should be able to ask 'what did my agent do today?' and get an answer. HMAC-chaining on secret lifecycle means the audit is tamper-evident, not just logged. OWASP compliance export matters for the Tinkerer persona running in an org context. Acceptance: every tool execution logged to JSONL; secret lifecycle events HMAC-chained; 'clawhq audit' shows recent activity; export produces OWASP-compatible format.
items.intent: Log every tool execution, secret access, and egress event to tamper-evident JSONL. The user must be able to answer 'what did my agent do?' at any time.

items.title: Export + destroy (data sovereignty)
items.description: Portable export with PII masking. Verified destruction with crypto proof.
items.groomingNotes: EXIT GUARANTEE: Privacy Migrant won't commit to a sovereign agent without knowing they can leave. 'clawhq export gives you everything portable. clawhq destroy proves it's gone. Zero lock-in.' — this is the product promise. PII masking on export means they can share the export without leaking data. Cryptographic proof of destruction is what makes 'destroy' trustworthy vs. just deleting files. Acceptance: export produces self-contained portable bundle; PII masked in export; destroy wipes all local data; crypto proof can be independently verified.
items.intent: Users must be able to export a portable agent bundle and cryptographically prove destruction. Zero lock-in — data sovereignty is the exit guarantee.

items.title: Approval queue
items.description: Runtime approval for high-stakes actions. Telegram integration. Resolution tracking.
items.groomingNotes: SAFE AUTOMATION: Without approval queue, the agent can't safely propose actions that have real-world consequences. The email-digest skill must propose draft replies for approval — not auto-send. Telegram integration means the user gets notified on their phone, not just in a terminal. Resolution tracking means the agent knows what was approved vs. rejected, and can learn from the pattern. Acceptance: high-stakes actions queue for approval; Telegram notification sent; user can approve/reject from Telegram; resolution recorded in audit trail.
items.intent: High-stakes agent actions must queue for user approval before execution. No auto-send, no auto-buy, no auto-delete without explicit user consent.

items.title: Status dashboard + logs + updates
items.description: clawhq status [--watch], clawhq logs, clawhq update [--check] with rollback.
items.groomingNotes: USER VALUE: Important day-2 ops visibility but not blocking first agent. Users need a working agent before they need to watch its status. Depends on CQ-003+CQ-012 anyway. Promote to must when ops track begins.
items.intent: Deliver status, logs, and update commands so operators can observe agent state, diagnose failures, and safely update without data loss.

items.title: Messaging channel connection
items.description: clawhq connect — Telegram, WhatsApp setup + health verification.
items.groomingNotes: HIGH VALUE: The last step before the user has a working agent. Without messaging channel, the agent exists but the user can't talk to it. Acceptance: 'clawhq connect' guides Telegram/WhatsApp bot token setup + verifies with live health ping. User receives a test message from their new agent.
items.intent: clawhq connect must configure and verify a messaging channel so the user can talk to their agent. Without this, the agent exists but is unreachable.

items.title: Quickstart (init -> build -> deploy -> verify)
items.description: clawhq quickstart [--blueprint] [--smart]. Phase-labeled spinners. No new logic.
items.groomingNotes: HIGH VALUE: The target user experience — one command to working agent. Phase-labeled spinners: Install -> Init -> Build -> Deploy -> Connect -> Verify. Missing dependency: also requires CQ-014 (install scaffold). Not ready until CQ-010+CQ-003+CQ-004+CQ-014 all land. Acceptance: 'clawhq quickstart --blueprint email-manager' produces a running, reachable agent with Telegram connected.
items.intent: clawhq quickstart must take a user from zero to a running, reachable agent in one command. Any failure must leave clear guidance, not a half-deployed mess.

items.title: Additional blueprint YAMLs (6)
items.description: Stock Trading, Meal Planner, Founder's Ops, Family Hub, AI Blog, Replace Google Assistant.
items.groomingNotes: BLUEPRINT LIBRARY: 6 additional blueprint YAMLs — Family Hub, Founder's Ops, Replace Google Assistant, Replace ChatGPT Plus, Replace my PA, Research Co-pilot. Validate against current blueprint schema (CQ-009), adapt naming/paths, add missing fields (email-manager-adjacent skills, cron). Email Manager (CQ-017) is separate and higher priority — do not include it here. Acceptance: 'clawhq blueprint list' shows 7+ blueprints; 'clawhq blueprint preview <each>' renders valid design.
items.intent: Ship 6 additional blueprint YAMLs that validate against the schema and render correctly in blueprint list/preview. Every blueprint must be forgeable.

items.title: Additional skills (5)
items.description: morning-brief, market-scan, meal-plan, investor-update, schedule-guard.
items.groomingNotes: BLUEPRINT COMPLETENESS: Skills are the value-add that make each blueprint useful. morning-brief is what makes 'Replace Google Assistant' actually replace Google Assistant. market-scan is why Stock Trading blueprint earns its name. Each skill is a standalone SKILL.md + prompts — thin to implement, high user impact. Acceptance: each skill installs via 'clawhq skill install'; SKILL.md + prompts present; skill runs on configured cron schedule.
items.intent: Ship 5 skills that install cleanly, run on cron, and make their respective blueprints actually useful. A blueprint without skills is an empty promise.

items.title: Tool installation framework
items.description: clawhq tool install/list/remove. Dockerfile patching. Stage 2 rebuild trigger.
items.groomingNotes: EXTENSIBILITY: Tool install framework is how users extend their agent's capabilities after initial setup. Without it, adding a new tool requires manual Dockerfile edits. Dockerfile patching + Stage 2 rebuild trigger means 'clawhq tool install' just works. Depends on CQ-003 (two-stage build) being complete. Acceptance: 'clawhq tool install <name>' adds tool to Dockerfile, triggers Stage 2 rebuild; 'clawhq tool list' shows installed tools; 'clawhq tool remove' cleanly uninstalls.
items.intent: clawhq tool install must add tools and trigger a Stage 2 rebuild automatically. Users must never hand-edit Dockerfiles to extend agent capabilities.

items.title: Notifications + alerts + self-repair + digest
items.description: Multi-channel notifications, predictive health alerts, auto-recovery, daily digest.
items.groomingNotes: DAY-2 OPS: Users who've had an agent for 30+ days need to know it's healthy without checking manually. Predictive health alerts catch problems before they become failures. Auto-recovery handles the common cases (container stopped, OOM) without user intervention. Daily digest gives the 'what did my agent do today?' answer proactively. Acceptance: health alerts fire before service goes down; auto-recovery handles restart + OOM cases; daily digest delivered via configured channel.
items.intent: Proactively alert users to agent health issues and auto-recover from common failures. Users should not discover their agent died by noticing silence.

items.title: AI config inference (--smart)
items.description: Ollama-powered natural language to blueprint + integrations.
items.groomingNotes: DELIGHT PATH: 'clawhq init --smart' is the < 5 minute path. Users describe what they need, Ollama infers blueprint + integrations. Local Ollama means zero data leaves. This is the product's wow moment. Correctly positioned as should — guided wizard (CQ-010) must work first as the fallback path. Acceptance: natural language description maps to correct blueprint; integration suggestions accurate; result produces same output as guided wizard; works fully locally with Ollama.
items.intent: clawhq init --smart must infer the right blueprint and integrations from natural language, fully local via Ollama. No data leaves the machine.

items.title: Memory lifecycle + autonomy + trace + learning
items.description: Hot/warm/cold tiers. Autonomy recommendations. Decision trace. Preference learning.
items.groomingNotes: LONG-TERM RETENTION: Memory lifecycle is what makes the agent smarter over time. Hot/warm/cold tiers prevent the 360KB memory bloat problem described in PRODUCT.md. Preference learning is the '6 months' story: 'It knows your top clients are always priority.' Not blocking initial adoption but critical for the 30-day churn metric. Acceptance: hot/warm/cold tiers active; LLM-powered summarization runs on schedule; PII masked before cold tier; agent preference patterns visible to user.
items.intent: Manage agent memory across hot/warm/cold tiers so the agent gets smarter over time without bloating context. PII must be masked before cold storage.

items.title: Migration import (ChatGPT + Google Assistant)
items.description: Parse exports, extract preferences via Ollama, map routines to cron, PII masking.
items.groomingNotes: MIGRATION STORY: Lowers the barrier for Privacy Migrants coming from ChatGPT or Google Assistant. Extracting preferences from export data means the forged agent already knows the user's patterns on day 1. PII masking during import keeps sensitive data local. Cron mapping from routines is the key value-add — not just importing preferences but translating them to scheduled jobs. Acceptance: ChatGPT export parsed; preferences extracted via Ollama; routine → cron mapping produced; PII masked; import completes without network calls.
items.intent: Import ChatGPT and Google Assistant exports so migrating users start with preferences intact on day 1. All processing local, PII masked.

items.title: Integration + provider management + roles
items.description: clawhq integrate, clawhq provider, clawhq role. Provider abstraction, credential routing, identity governance.
items.groomingNotes: INTEGRATION MANAGEMENT: As agents grow (Month 1-6 story), users add integrations. Integration management makes this clean vs. ad-hoc file editing. Provider abstraction means credential routing is automatic. Identity governance (roles) matters for Fleet Operator persona managing agents for multiple people. Acceptance: 'clawhq integrate' connects new service with live validation; 'clawhq provider' manages cloud API routing; role definitions control what each integration can access.
items.intent: Deliver integration, provider, and role management so users add services and govern access through commands, not config file edits.

items.title: Evolve rollback + credentials.json + posture.yaml
items.description: Rollback snapshots for all capability changes. Separate credential store. Security posture file.
items.groomingNotes: SECURITY MATURITY: credentials.json separation from .env is the difference between 'works' and 'production-grade'. Integration credentials in a dedicated store (mode 0600) means rotate one integration without touching others. Security posture YAML makes the container hardening visible and reviewable — the Tinkerer persona wants to see what posture they're running. Rollback snapshots for all capability changes complete the safety story started in CQ-021. Acceptance: credentials.json separate from .env; posture.yaml reflects active security settings; rollback restores previous capability state.
items.intent: Separate credentials into credentials.json (mode 0600), expose security posture in posture.yaml, and ensure every capability change has a rollback snapshot.

items.title: Trust modes + heartbeat + command queue
items.description: Paranoid/zero-trust/managed. Kill switch. Outbound health reporter. Signed command queue.
items.groomingNotes: MANAGED MODE FOUNDATION: Trust modes are what makes the cloud layer safe by design. Paranoid users run fully offline. Zero-Trust users get optional remote monitoring without any inbound access. Managed users get operational automation with content architecturally blocked. Kill switch ('clawhq cloud disconnect') with no confirmation prompt is the user's emergency brake. Signed command queue prevents malicious commands even if cloud is compromised. Acceptance: all three trust modes switch cleanly; kill switch disconnects immediately; command signature verified before execution; unsupported commands rejected in all modes.
items.intent: Implement three trust modes so users choose their privacy level. Kill switch must disconnect immediately. Signed command queue must reject tampered commands.

items.title: Fleet management
items.description: Multi-agent discovery, health aggregation, fleet-wide doctor.
items.groomingNotes: FLEET OPERATOR: Multi-agent discovery + fleet-wide doctor is for the Fleet Operator persona managing agents for multiple people. Single pane of glass: see which agents are healthy, which need attention. Fleet-wide doctor means one check to confirm all agents are configured correctly. Not blocking single-user adoption. Acceptance: 'clawhq cloud fleet' discovers running agents; fleet health shows aggregate status; fleet doctor surfaces issues across all agents.
items.intent: Fleet management must discover all running agents and aggregate health into a single view. Fleet-wide doctor must surface issues across all agents in one check.

items.title: Web dashboard (server + 7 pages)
items.description: Hono + htmx + Pico CSS. Home, doctor, logs, deploy, approvals, skills, init wizard.
items.groomingNotes: WEB UI OPTION: Hono + htmx + Pico CSS keeps the stack minimal. Web dashboard is for users who prefer a browser to a terminal for day-to-day ops. Not blocking CLI users. Depends on CQ-011 (doctor), CQ-026 (status), CQ-025 (approvals) being solid first — web is just a UI layer over CLI-complete features. Acceptance: all 7 pages render; doctor page shows same output as CLI; approvals queue shows pending items; init wizard works via browser.
items.intent: Web dashboard must expose full operational control — doctor, logs, approvals, init — not a read-only view. Browser users get the same capability as CLI users.

items.title: Air-gapped mode
items.description: Zero egress at config + firewall level. Doctor verifies rules active.
items.groomingNotes: PARANOID MODE: Air-gapped deployment for users who want zero network access from the agent. Config-level (zero egress in openclaw.json) + firewall-level (CLAWHQ_FWD blocks all outbound) hardening in combination. Doctor verifies both layers are active. Use case: sensitive environments, compliance requirements, maximum privacy. Depends on CQ-005 (firewall) and CQ-010 (wizard) being complete first. Acceptance: air-gap mode sets zero egress in config; firewall blocks all outbound; doctor confirms air-gap active; agent functional without any network access.
items.intent: Air-gapped mode must block all egress at config and firewall layers. Doctor must verify both layers active. The agent must function without any network access.

items.title: Smoke test + backing services
items.description: Post-deploy verification. clawhq service add postgres/redis/qdrant.
items.groomingNotes: POST-DEPLOY CONFIDENCE: Smoke test is the final step in 'clawhq up' that tells the user their agent is actually reachable, not just running. Without it, 'deploy succeeded' could mean the container started but the agent isn't responding. Backing services (postgres/redis/qdrant) unblock power users who want persistent memory or vector search. Acceptance: smoke test sends real message to agent and gets response; 'clawhq service add postgres' configures and starts service; backing services health-checked by doctor.
items.intent: Post-deploy smoke test must send a real message and get a response — 'container started' is not 'agent works'. Backing services must be health-checked by doctor.

items.title: clawhq blueprint list + preview commands
items.description: CLI surface for browsing blueprints before committing to setup. 'clawhq blueprint list' shows available blueprints with one-line descriptions. 'clawhq blueprint preview <name>' shows full blueprint design: identity, tools, skills, cron, integrations, security posture, egress domains. Builds on CQ-009 (blueprint loader). Required for informed user choice before init.
items.groomingNotes: HIGH VALUE: Gate for informed blueprint selection. Without clawhq blueprint list/preview, users run init blind — they can't evaluate options before committing. Pairs tightly with CQ-009 (loader already does the heavy lifting). Build immediately after CQ-009. Acceptance: 'clawhq blueprint list' shows all available blueprints with one-line descriptions; 'clawhq blueprint preview email-manager' renders full design (identity, tools, skills, cron, integrations, security posture, egress domains). No net-new logic — thin CLI surface over CQ-009.
items.intent: clawhq blueprint list and preview must let users evaluate every available blueprint before committing to init. Users must never choose a blueprint blind.

_sprint_order: Foundation (CQ-001,002,013) first — everything depends on config types + gateway + CLI. Then build+secure in parallel. Design track: CQ-009 (blueprint loader) → FEAT-001 (blueprint list/preview CLI) → CQ-010 (init wizard) → CQ-017 (Email Manager blueprint) → CQ-019+020 (identity+tools). Operate depends on build. Evolve after foundation. Cloud last. CQ-029 blueprint YAMLs already in configs/templates/ — reuse them.
